server, creates a socket, and accepts connections.</div>" div.div:
both communicate with the previous game
classes as it was done in previous tasks. Only, there is no Player class called directly. The logic previously handeled by Player is now duplicated into ControlledPlayer and RemotePlayer, both of which extend from Player

div.div:
The player sub class that takes inputs and performs it like the old player.
Additionally sends the new rotation of the player
It creates a new socket and binds to a specific server address (hostname + port)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 206px; height: 1px; padding-top: 458px; margin-left: 1049px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #23445D;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#23445D, #a0bcd2); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

div.div:
Receives the rotation from the Remote subclass and performs an action based on the received value.
Does not check for inputs
itself, does not do anything else.
It creates the server, creates a socket, and accepts connections.
